seo.title: Building Code Generation with Pydantic AI + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Pydantic AI + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Pydantic AI enables type-safe LLM agents for production code generation, combining structured outputs with agentic workflows for iterative refinement. Guardrails are essential to mitigate risks like malicious code injection, syntax errors, credential leaks, and sandbox escapes in high-stakes environments. Vex provides runtime verification that catches these failures before deployment impact.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Pydantic AI
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with a Pydantic AI agent receiving requirements and generating initial code via structured LLM calls. The generated code is sandboxed using restricted Python execution environments (like restrictedpython or Docker), validated against test suites, and scanned for safety violations. Failed validations trigger iterative refinement loops where the agent debugs and regenerates code.

Tool calling handles sandbox execution results, security scans, and test outcomes as structured Pydantic models. Streaming responses enable real-time validation during generation. The result_validator pattern intercepts all agent outputs for Vex verification before they reach the application layer.

Dependency injection provides test fixtures, sandbox configs, and credential vaults to nested tool calls, ensuring context preservation across refinement cycles.

risks.name: Malicious Code Injection
risks.description: LLM generates code with syscalls, file writes, or network requests that escape sandbox boundaries
risks.mitigation: Vex static analysis + dynamic sandbox execution with syscall filtering

risks.name: Credential Hardcoding
risks.description: Generated code embeds API keys, passwords, or secrets from training data leakage
risks.mitigation: Vex regex pattern matching + semantic credential detection

risks.name: Syntax/Import Errors
risks.description: Invalid Python syntax or unavailable dependencies cause runtime failures post-generation
risks.mitigation: Vex AST parsing + restricted import validation

risks.name: Infinite Refinement Loops
risks.description: Tool retry logic masks persistent validation failures, consuming quota without progress
risks.mitigation: Vex failure pattern detection + max iteration enforcement

implementation.title: Basic Code Generation Agent
implementation.description: Define Pydantic models and agent with code generation tool using structured outputs
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import Agent, tool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class CodeRequest(BaseModel):
    requirements: str = Field(..., description="What the code should do")
    language: str = Field(default="python")

class CodeResponse(BaseModel):
    code: str
    explanation: str

@tool
async def generate_code(request: CodeRequest) -> CodeResponse:
    """Generate production-ready code from requirements."""
    agent = Agent(model="gpt-4o")
    response = await agent.run(f"Generate {request.language} code for: {request.requirements}")
    return CodeResponse(code=response.content, explanation="Generated code")

code_agent = Agent(tools=[generate_code])

implementation.title: Sandbox Execution Tool
implementation.description: Execute generated code in restricted environment with timeout and memory limits

implementation.title: Test Suite Validator
implementation.description: Validate generated code against unit tests and security patterns

implementation.title: Iterative Refinement Loop
implementation.description: Main agent orchestrating code generation → sandbox → validation → refinement

vexIntegration.title: Vex Result Validator on Agent
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as result_validator to catch all agent output failures before application consumption
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk import VexClient
from pydantic_ai import Agent

# Initialize Vex client
vex = VexClient(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

# Define code generation guardrail
code_safety_suite = {
    "checks": [
        "code_safety.static_analysis",
        "code_safety.credential_leakage",
        "code_safety.syntax_validation",
        "code_safety.dependency_safety"
    ],
    "sandbox_execution": True,
    "max_iterations": 5
}

# Enhanced agent with Vex validation
secure_agent = Agent(
    tools=[generate_code, execute_sandbox, validate_code],
    result_validator=lambda output: vex.verify(
        payload=output,
        suite_id="code-generation-high-risk",
        context={"requirements": user_request}
    )
)

# Usage
result = await secure_agent.run(user_request)
# Vex validation happens automatically before result is returned

vexIntegration.title: Custom Vex Check for Credential Leaks
vexIntegration.description: Define custom Vex check specifically for code generation credential patterns

vexIntegration.title: Streaming Validation with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Validate code chunks during streaming generation to catch issues mid-generation
vexIntegration.code:
from vex_sdk.streaming import StreamingVerifier

async def stream_validated_code(requirements: str):
    verifier = StreamingVerifier(
        suite_id="code-generation-streaming",
        chunk_size=512  # Validate every 512 tokens
    )
    
    agent = Agent(streaming=True)
    
    async for chunk in agent.stream(requirements):
        # Send code chunk to Vex for partial validation
        validation = await verifier.validate_chunk(chunk)
        
        if not validation.passed:
            # Early termination on critical issues
            if validation.severity == "critical":
                raise ValueError(f"Generation rejected: {validation.issues}")
            
        yield chunk
    
    # Final validation
    final_result = await verifier.finalize()
    if not final_result.passed:
        raise ValueError("Final validation failed")

# Usage
async for code_chunk in stream_validated_code("Generate login handler"):
    print(code_chunk, end='')
